article_title: US Measles Cases Hit 1,952 as Country Edges Toward Losing Elimination Status It Has Held Since 2000
summary: All integrity checks pass. PAHO (paho.org) is the WHO's regional office for the Americas and a credible primary source; the allowlist warning is overridden. All quotes, statistics, and claims verified against source snapshots.

findings.message: Source fetched via Archive.org fallback (original returned 200)

content_preview.title: US Measles Cases Hit 1,952 as Country Edges Toward Losing Elimination Status It Has Held Since 2000
content_preview.summary: The CDC logged 1,952 measles cases through May 21, 2026 — on pace to surpass last year's high not seen since 1992 — as a Lancet study concludes four of seven elimination indicators have already been missed.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

The United States has recorded 1,952 confirmed measles cases in 2026 as of May 21, according to the [Centers for Disease Control and Prevention](https://www.cdc.gov/measles/data-research/index.html) — a total that places the country on pace to surpass 2025's tally of 2,288 cases, the highest annual count since 1992. A research letter published April 30 in *The Lancet* concluded that four of the seven CDC indicators for measles elimination have already been missed and that, given the...

recommendations: Consider adding paho.org to config/source_allowlist.txt — PAHO is the WHO's regional office for the Americas and a routine primary source for public health stories.

editor_notes.content_quality: Well-structured News article at 971 words, within the 400–1200 word range for the category. The four-section structure (Overview, What We Know, What We Don't Know, Analysis) is clear and professionally written. The analysis section appropriately contextualizes the significance without editorializing.
editor_notes.source_verification: All four source snapshots read and verified. source-0.html.gz (CDC, cdc.gov): Confirms 1,952 cases as of May 21 in 40 jurisdictions, 29 outbreaks, 93% outbreak-associated, 48 outbreaks and 2,288 total cases in 2025, and MMR vaccination decline from 95.2% (2019-20) to 92.5% (2024-25). source-1.html.gz (Healthline, May 14 snapshot): Confirms 92% of 2026 cases unvaccinated or unknown status, 93% of 2025 cases unvaccinated or unknown, 3 deaths and 245 hospitalizations in 2025, Nathan Lo and William Schaffner quotes verified verbatim. Note: Healthline's May 14 snapshot shows 2,242 for 2025 total — the article uses 2,288 from the authoritative CDC source, which is correct (Healthline was working from earlier data). source-2.html.gz (Medical Xpress via Archive.org fallback, April 30 snapshot): Confirms Lancet analysis by Majumder and Bischops, 93 cases per 10 million people as of early 2026, 6-7% cases from abroad, Rt exceeded 1.0 more than 75% of time since early 2025, Texas vaccination rates 79-90%, both researcher quotes verified verbatim. source-3.html.gz (PAHO, paho.org): Confirms November 2026 review date, January 20, 2025 analysis start date, Canada lost status in November 2025 after 12+ months uninterrupted circulation, US and Mexico face individual reviews. PAHO is the WHO's regional office for the Americas — a primary authoritative source for this story; the allowlist warning is overridden by editorial judgment.
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: All statistical claims verified: 1,952 cases (CDC), 40 jurisdictions (CDC), 29 outbreaks (CDC), 93% outbreak-associated (CDC), 2,288 2025 total (CDC), 92% 2026 unvaccinated/unknown (Healthline), 93% 2025 unvaccinated/unknown (Healthline), 3 deaths 245 hospitalizations in 2025 (Healthline), 93 cases per 10 million in early 2026 (Medical Xpress/Lancet), 6-7% from abroad (Medical Xpress/Lancet), Rt exceeded 1.0 more than 75% of time (Medical Xpress/Lancet), 95.2%-to-92.5% vaccination decline (CDC), Texas rates 79-90% (Medical Xpress/Lancet), November 2026 PAHO review (PAHO), January 20, 2025 start date (PAHO), Canada lost status November 2025 (PAHO). The article uses 'research letter' in Overview and 'correspondence letter' in The Lancet Analysis section for the same Lancet piece — a minor internal inconsistency but not a factual error.
editor_notes.tone_assessment: Neutral and professional throughout. No sensationalism. The analysis section correctly qualifies the November PAHO review as 'not a final verdict' and acknowledges the genome-sequencing pathway. No AI self-reference. Language is measured and factual.
editor_notes.originality: No duplicate coverage found. Grep search of src/content/articles/ confirms no prior measles articles have been published. This is the first measles story in the archive.
recommendations: Consider adding paho.org to config/source_allowlist.txt for future health stories citing PAHO data.
editor_notes.overall_assessment: High-quality, well-sourced News article on a genuinely significant public health story. All four sources read, all claims verified. No fabrications, misattributed quotes, or unsourced specifics detected. Ready for publication.
